title: Buttery Crumbly Jamaican Mushrooms Fritters
description: A quick-fix Jamaican dish featuring wild mushrooms, with a buttery and crumbly texture. This Mediterranean-friendly recipe is perfect for a snack or appetizer.

ingredients.name: Wild mushrooms (such as chanterelle or cremini)

instructions: Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan to 375°F (190°C).
In a large bowl, combine the sliced mushrooms, flour, breadcrumbs, jerk seasoning, baking powder, and salt. Mix well.
Add the beaten egg to the bowl and mix until the ingredients are well combined.
Add the melted butter to the bowl and mix until the ingredients are well combined.
Using your hands, shape the mixture into small patties, about 1 1/2 inches (3.8 cm) in diameter and 1/2 inch (1.3 cm) thick.
Carefully place the patties in the hot oil and fry for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the fritters from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the fritters hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
